executive_summary: OpenAI has recently released GPT-5.4, a significant upgrade to their existing models designed for professional work. This model integrates advanced coding capabilities and improved reasoning abilities, making it more efficient in handling complex tasks. According to official sources, GPT-5.4 is the most capable and efficient frontier model for professional use, with enhanced features such as upfront thinking plans and native computer-use capabilities. Trusted sources highlight its efficiency gains, particularly in terms of token usage and web browsing capabilities, while also noting concerns about potential job displacement due to advanced automation.
conflict_and_consensus: Both official and trusted sources agree that GPT-5.4 is a major advancement for OpenAI's professional models, emphasizing improvements in reasoning, coding, and computer-use capabilities. However, there are differing views on the practical implications of these advancements. Official sources focus more on the technical achievements, while trusted sources also discuss potential impacts on employment.

official_insights.title: GPT-5.4 Release Overview
official_insights.brief_summary: OpenAI has introduced GPT-5.4, a model designed for professional work that integrates advanced coding and reasoning capabilities. It is available in ChatGPT (as GPT-5.4 Thinking) and the API, with enhanced features like upfront thinking plans and improved deep web research.
official_insights.reasoning: The article details the specific enhancements of GPT-5.4, such as its ability to provide an upfront plan during responses and better handling of complex tasks, which directly answers the query about OpenAI's latest product.

trusted_insights.title: GPT-5.4's Native Computer Use Mode
trusted_insights.brief_summary: OpenAI has released GPT-5.4 with a new 'native' computer-use mode, allowing the model to navigate users' computers like a human and work across applications. This feature is particularly significant for enterprise use cases.
trusted_insights.reasoning: The article highlights the introduction of native computer-use capabilities in GPT-5.4, which is a key advancement that directly addresses the user's query about OpenAI's latest product.
key_features: ability to operate computers and carry out complex workflows across applications

references.title: Introducing GPT-5.4 - OpenAI
references.snippet: Introducing GPT-5.4, OpenAI's most most capable and efficient frontier model for professional work, with state-of-the-art coding,

references.title: OpenAI launches GPT-5.4 with native computer use mode, financial plugins for Microsoft Excel, Google Sheets - VentureBeat
references.snippet:
OpenAI also links computer use to improvements in vision and document handling. On MMMU-Pro, GPT-5.4 reaches 81.2% success without tool use, compared with 79.5% for GPT-5.2, and OpenAI says it achieves that result using a fraction of the “thinking tokens.”

On OmniDocBench, GPT-5.4’s average error is reported at 0.109, improved from 0.140 for GPT-5.2. The post also describes expanded support for high-fidelity image inputs, including an “original” detail level up to 10.24M pixels.

OpenAI positions GPT-5.4 as built for longer, multi-step workflows—work that increasingly looks like an agent keeping state across many actions rather than a chatbot responding once.

## Tool search and improved tool orchestration [...] OpenAI describes the efficiency win with a concrete comparison: on 250 tasks from Scale’s MCP Atlas benchmark, running with 36 MCP servers enabled, the tool-search configuration reduced total token usage by 47% while achieving the same accuracy as a configuration that exposed all MCP functions directly in context.

That 47% figure is specifically about the tool-search setup in that evaluation—not a blanket claim that GPT-5.4 uses 47% fewer tokens for every kind of task.

## Improvements for developers and coding workflows

OpenAI’s coding pitch is that GPT-5.4 combines the coding strengths of GPT-5.3-Codex with stronger tool and computer-use capabilities that matter when tasks aren’t single-shot. [...] ChatGPT Free users will also get a taste of GPT-5.4, but only when their queries are auto-routed to the model, according to an OpenAI spokesperson.

The big headlines on this release are efficiency, with OpenAI reporting that GPT-5.4 uses far fewer tokens (47% fewer on some tasks) than its predecessors, and, arguably even more impressively, a new "native" Computer Use mode available through the API and its Codex that lets GPT-5.4 navigate a users' computer like a human and work across applications.
